extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC Primoz won Stage 17 with VINGEGAARD Jonas on his wheel, burning matches for personal glory while teammate KUSS Sepp defended red by 19 seconds, preserving an 8-second GC lead.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC prioritized stage win over team preservation with 100m sprint against VINGEGAARD, risking KUSS's red jersey for personal palmares.
EVENEPOEL Remco conceded massive time previous day wearing rain jacket entire stage, securing freedom for future breakaway attempts from jumbo oversight.
UAE sent SOLER Marc early attack before main climb to force jumbo pacing, revealing team depth strategy against numerically superior opponent.
KUSS held podium position by only 19 seconds after teammates took time bonuses, exposing leader vulnerability when domestiques chase glory.
LANDA Mikel closed gap on descent after being dropped on steep section, demonstrating technical skill compensates for climbing deficit on Angliru.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: Won stage with second-fastest Angliru ascent ever, but prioritizing victory over team tactics signals confidence or internal tension.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Followed ROGLIC wheel to second place, taking 6-second time bonus that directly threatened own teammate KUSS's red jersey.
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: neutral: Retained red jersey by only 8 seconds after teammates took bonuses, fighting desperately on steep gradients suggesting defensive vulnerability remains.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Strategic time concession previous stage wearing rain jacket bought freedom for breakaway hunting, suggesting calculated GC sacrifice for stage opportunities.
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: Deployed as early danger attack by UAE before main climb to force jumbo pacing response, tactical weapon despite GC position.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: Descended technical roads after being dropped to close back within striking distance, revealing descending as weapon on technical finishes.
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: Lost significant time on Angliru steep sections, dropping from comfortable fourth to 4+ minutes deficit, exposing inexperience on extreme gradients.
